tasks.description: Restructure system prompt with triage and add MESSAGE to OUTPUT_RULES in templates.ts. (1) Change the system message opening from 'You are a Remotion custom effect generator' to 'You are an AI assistant for a video effect creation tool' with a TRIAGE block defining QUESTION MODE and EFFECT MODE with examples. Scope all existing technical rules (EFFECT_COMPONENT_CONTRACT, AVAILABLE_GLOBALS, OUTPUT_RULES, VALIDATION_RULES) under an 'EFFECT MODE rules' header. (2) Add '// MESSAGE: <brief note>' to OUTPUT_RULES after the PARAMS line (after line 68). (3) Change the user message from 'Build a ${category} Remotion effect...' to neutral 'User request for a ${category} effect...' framing. Retry/fix mode keeps its existing code-generation framing unchanged since retries are inherently code-fixing. Key lines: systemMsg construction at 173-181, userMsg at 183-197, OUTPUT_RULES at 60-106.

Add QUESTION_RESPONSE and MESSAGE extraction functions in templates.ts. (1) Add QUESTION_RESPONSE_MARKER regex and MESSAGE_PATTERN regex near existing patterns at line 202-204. (2) Add exported extractQuestionResponse() function that checks for // QUESTION_RESPONSE marker and returns {isQuestion: true, message: string} or null. Uses stripMarkdownFences, extracts everything after the marker line as the message. (3) Add extractMessage() helper that extracts // MESSAGE: single-line metadata. (4) Update ExtractedEffectMeta interface (line 22-27) to add 'message: string'. (5) Update extractEffectCodeAndMeta() (line 365-388) to extract message, strip its range from code, and include it in the return value. No changes to validateExtractedEffectCode — MESSAGE is just carried along on success, validation still throws on malformed code.

Update edge function response shape in index.ts. (1) Import extractQuestionResponse from ./templates.ts (line 10-14). (2) After the raw output log (line 300) and before the existing extractEffectCodeAndMeta try-catch (line 302), add a question-response check: call extractQuestionResponse(llmResponse.content), and if it returns non-null, log and return jsonResponse({message, isQuestionResponse: true, model}). This runs before code extraction so question responses never enter validation/retry. (3) Update the successful code response (line 358-364) to include 'message: extracted.message || undefined'. Critical: question check must be BEFORE the try-catch at line 302, not inside it.
Deploy and validate edge function. Run: npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc. Verify deployment succeeds without errors.
commands_run: npx supabase functions deploy ai-generate-effect --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc

tasks.description: Update frontend EffectCreatorPanel.tsx to handle and display agent messages. (1) Update GenerateEffectResponse interface (line 54-60): make 'code' optional (code?: string), add 'message?: string' and 'isQuestionResponse?: boolean'. (2) Add state: const [agentMessage, setAgentMessage] = useState<string | null>(null) near line 194. (3) Import X icon from lucide-react (line 2, alongside existing imports like Sparkles). (4) Update resetForm (line 206-222) to include setAgentMessage(null). (5) Update handleGenerate (line 264-316): clear previous message at top (setAgentMessage(null) after abort setup), then after receiving response, branch on isQuestionResponse — if true, set agentMessage, clear prompt, and return early (existing code/name/schema untouched for edit mode). For normal responses, set agentMessage from response.message and continue existing logic. Guard response.code access since it's now optional. (6) Add inline message UI between preview section (ends ~line 515) and prompt area (~line 524): a dismissible box with Sparkles icon, message text, and X dismiss button. Use 'flex gap-2 rounded-lg border border-border bg-muted/40 px-3 py-2' styling. Only render when agentMessage is truthy and !isGenerating.

Run TypeScript type-check and any existing tests to verify changes compile and don't break existing behavior. Run: npx tsc --noEmit (or project's type-check command). Check for type errors in templates.ts, index.ts, and EffectCreatorPanel.tsx. If type errors exist, fix them and re-run. Also verify with a quick grep that no other files import ExtractedEffectMeta or GenerateEffectResponse that would break from the interface changes.

watch_items: QUESTION_RESPONSE check in index.ts MUST run before the extractEffectCodeAndMeta try-catch — if placed inside the catch block, question responses would be misclassified as extraction failures

Malformed code with // MESSAGE: but no // QUESTION_RESPONSE must still flow through validation → retry. The two markers are independent signals — MESSAGE is metadata on code responses, QUESTION_RESPONSE is a binary gate for conversational answers
Retry/fix mode (retryDepth > 0) should keep its existing code-generation framing — never question-route a retry since retries are inherently fixing broken code
In edit-mode question responses, existing code/name/schema/previewComponent state in EffectCreatorPanel must NOT be overwritten — the early return skips all code-update logic
GenerateEffectResponse.code becomes optional (code?: string) — guard all downstream accesses (response.code!) to avoid runtime errors on question-only responses

The system prompt restructuring changes the LLM's persona from 'Remotion custom effect generator' to 'AI assistant for a video effect creation tool' — verify this doesn't degrade code generation quality
stripMarkdownFences is called in extractQuestionResponse — ensure the QUESTION_RESPONSE marker regex works on both raw and fence-stripped text
Debt watch: no overlap with any tracked debt items — this change is additive to the edge function and frontend panel

sense_checks.question: Does the restructured system prompt clearly separate QUESTION MODE and EFFECT MODE with the triage block at the top level (not buried in OUTPUT_RULES)? Does the user message use neutral 'User request for' framing instead of 'Build a'?

Are extractQuestionResponse() and extractMessage() completely independent functions? Does extractEffectCodeAndMeta still call validateExtractedEffectCode unconditionally (no short-circuit on MESSAGE presence)?
Is the questionResponse check placed BEFORE the extractEffectCodeAndMeta try-catch (not inside it)? Does a question-only response return immediately without entering the code extraction/retry path?
Did the edge function deploy succeed without errors? Are both templates.ts and index.ts changes included in the deployment?

When isQuestionResponse is true, does handleGenerate return early WITHOUT modifying code/name/parameterSchema/previewComponent state? Is the agentMessage dismissed on new generation, form reset, and X button click?
Does tsc --noEmit pass with zero errors? Are there any other consumers of ExtractedEffectMeta or GenerateEffectResponse that need updating?

meta_commentary:
Execution order is strictly sequential T1→T2→T3→T4, then T5 can parallel with T4 (both depend on T3), and T6 gates on both T4 and T5.

Key judgment calls:
- T1 and T2 are split because T1 (prompt restructuring) is conceptually distinct from T2 (extraction logic) even though both edit templates.ts. The executor could merge them into one pass if preferred.
- T5 (frontend) is deliberately independent of T4 (deploy) since the frontend changes don't require the deployed function to compile — they just need the response shape to be agreed upon.
- The plan's retry/fix mode intentionally skips the question-response path. The executor should verify this by tracing: when retryDepth > 0, the question check still runs on the LLM response, but the retry prompt's strong code-fixing framing makes it extremely unlikely the LLM would output QUESTION_RESPONSE. If this is a concern, the executor could add an explicit `if (retryDepth > 0)` guard before the question check, but the plan doesn't call for it.
- The Sparkles icon is already imported in EffectCreatorPanel.tsx (line 2), so only X needs to be added.
- The edge function runs in Deno (supabase/functions/), not Node — ensure any TypeScript patterns are Deno-compatible (they are, since we're just using regex and string manipulation).

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Restructure system prompt with triage
Step 2: Add QUESTION_RESPONSE and MESSAGE extraction
Step 3: Update edge function response shape
Step 4: Deploy and validate edge function
Step 5: Update response type and state in EffectCreatorPanel
Step 6: Add inline message UI in EffectCreatorPanel

validation.completeness_notes: T6 (type-check/test) is the mandatory verification task not explicitly in the plan but required by execution protocol. Steps 5 and 6 from the plan are merged into T5 since they both edit EffectCreatorPanel.tsx and are tightly coupled (state changes + UI that consumes that state). All 6 plan steps are covered.
